pairs.question: When and where was Dallas Eliuk born?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk was born on November 4, 1964, in Vancouver, British Columbia.

pairs.question: What positions did Dallas Eliuk play in professional lacrosse?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk was a professional box lacrosse goaltender.

pairs.question: How many years did Dallas Eliuk play professionally, and for which teams?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk played professionally for 18 years. He played 15 seasons for the Philadelphia Wings, was traded to the Portland LumberJax (where he played for three seasons), and later played for the Colorado Mammoth as a coach.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Eliuk accomplish during his lacrosse career?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk won four championships with the Philadelphia Wings, earned two championship MVP awards, was named to the All-Pro team nine times, and retired as the National Lacrosse League's all-time leader in saves.

pairs.question: How many times was Dallas Eliuk inducted into the Lacrosse Hall of Fame?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk was inducted into the Canadian Lacrosse Hall of Fame in November 2013 and the National Lacrosse League Hall of Fame in March 2009.

pairs.question: Describe Dallas Eliuk's participation in 'The Price Is Right' and the prizes he won.
pairs.answer: In January 2005, while the Philadelphia Wings were in Anaheim for a game, Eliuk and several teammates attended a taping of 'The Price Is Right.' Eliuk was selected to play and won a world-map lithograph, some dinnerware, and an exercise machine. The episode aired on February 11, 2005.

pairs.question: Who is Dallas Eliuk married to, and when was their first child born?
pairs.answer: Dallas Eliuk is married to former radio DJ Daria O'Neill. Their first child, Xander Eliuk, was born in May 2009.
